test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: The strong Zipfian distribution (RÂ²=0.9084) and preserved word frequency patterns are consistent with a substitution cipher on natural language, as substitution preserves the rank-frequency structure of the underlying language. However, these statistics alone cannot distinguish between an actual cipher and unenciphered natural text.

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Zipf exponent and word length are reasonable for natural language, but the 70.1% hapax ratio far exceeds typical natural language patterns (40-60%), suggesting the text is not a simple substitution cipher on a known language.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The manuscript's text structure reflects a single, coherent narrative.

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: High hapax ratio (0.701) and low Zipf exponent (0.8946) contradict coherent narrative structure; natural language narratives show higher repetition and steeper frequency distributions.
